notes: Python 1.5.15 — two MoE throughput hotfixes on top of 1.5.14. (1) Hybrid SSM models (Qwen3.5/3.6 MoE, Nemotron-H, Gemma 4 hybrid) no longer hit `UnboundLocalError: SSM_REDERIVE_MIN_TOKENS` in scheduler._cleanup_finished on every non-thinking turn — constants hoisted to module scope so both `_gpl > 0` and `_gpl == 0` branches see them. Live-verified Qwen3.5-35B-A3B-JANG_4K and Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-JANGTQ4 went from ~60 t/s (with cleanup error firing per request) to 70-78 t/s (clean). (2) Big MoE bundles (MiniMax 38 GB JANGTQ2, etc.) no longer SIGKILL during decode: jang_loader._set_wired_limit_for_model headroom bumped from a flat 8 GB to max(16 GB, 30 % of model size). 8 GB was tight for routed-expert dequant + KV + Metal scratch on first inference; the new heuristic stays under max_recommended_working_set on M-series with ≥96 GB. MiniMax-M2.7-Small-JANGTQ now decodes cleanly at 39 t/s instead of being killed by the kernel on first prompt. All four API protocols inherit both fixes via the shared scheduler/loader path.
